target: WIN-070 detail_evidence in data/wins.json — single sentence-scope insertion in point (3) closing the orbital-mechanics moderate hole flagged in WIN-070.c1.json. Pre-empts the temporal-correlation rescue inline so a reader who never clicks through to detail_extra sees the rescue closed in the main argument. TLDRs, detail_verdict_text, finding, code_analysis, and all other fields are unchanged by this EXP.
source_issue: ISS-2074 (moderate, c1 hole: orbital-mechanics framing leaves temporal-correlation rescue open in main text)
patch_instructions: One atomic append to the end of detail_evidence point (3) in WIN-070 (id="070") in data/wins.json. The BEFORE string is the closing sentence of point (3) and is unique (count=1). After integration, verify: (a) the new outbound link https://podaac-tools.jpl.nasa.gov/drive/files/allData/grace/docs/GRACE_L1A_TM_JPL_v1.0.pdf is already present in the WIN (it is cited in point (1) and in detail_verdict_text) so it is not a new domain; (b) the new outbound link https://podaac.jpl.nasa.gov/dataset/GRACE_L1A_GRAV_JPL_RL03 is also already present in point (1) — no new domains are introduced. No change to TLDRs, detail_verdict_text, finding, or code_analysis. Projected new detail_evidence length: ~6790 chars (current 5927, delta ~+863).

patch_anchors.before: Whatever the chart's bottom panel shows, it cannot be GRACE measurements co-located with BOU for the full window.
patch_anchors.after: Whatever the chart's bottom panel shows, it cannot be GRACE measurements co-located with BOU for the full window. A defender may try to rescue this by reading the chart as a temporal correlation between Boulder's magnetometer and a globally-averaged (not spatially co-located) GRACE measurement, but that rescue collides with the dome's own framing on two counts. First, "phase-locked" is the dome's own term and denotes a spatial-temporal coupling — a strictly-temporal rescue therefore contradicts the chart's headline framing. Second, even the strictly-temporal reading at 1-second cadence over a 27-minute window requires explicit telemetry-timing reconciliation against the L1A epoch tags, sampling cadence, and downsampling schedule documented in JPL D-22027 (backup landing page: PO.DAAC GRACE_L1A_GRAV_JPL_RL03) — the chart neither addresses nor cites this reconciliation.
patch_anchors.rationale: Closes WIN-070.c1 hole (moderate): the original point (3) framing — "phase-locked co-temporal alignment with a fixed point at BOU is physically possible for at most ~2 minutes per pass" — leaves open the rescue "the chart shows temporal correlation between Boulder and a globally-averaged GRACE quantity during the same UT window, not spatial co-location." The detail_extra anticipated-rescue paragraph (objection ii in detail_extra: "globally-averaged GRACE Level-3") addresses this, but a reader who never clicks through sees an attack the dome can shrug off as a strawman. The patch appends one sentence that (a) names the rescue and (b) closes it on two grounds: (i) the term "phase-locked" — which is the dome's own framing on the home page — is a spatial-temporal coupling term, so the strictly-temporal reading contradicts the dome's adjacent framing; (ii) a strictly-temporal reading at 1-second cadence over 27 minutes still requires GRACE L1A timing reconciliation (epoch tags, sampling cadence, downsampling schedule per JPL D-22027), which the chart neither addresses nor cites. The 27-minute-window infeasibility from the original sentence remains as the strongest, geometry-independent leg of the attack — the new content does not replace it, it pre-empts a sub-rescue that would otherwise dodge it. Both JPL URLs cited are already present in the WIN (used in point (1) and detail_verdict_text); the patch does not introduce new outbound domains.

holes_addressed.hole: WIN-070.c1 moderate hole / ISS-2074 (orbital-mechanics temporal-correlation rescue open in main text)
holes_addressed.description: detail_evidence point (3) currently argues "phase-locked co-temporal alignment with a fixed point at BOU is physically possible for at most ~2 minutes per pass — not for a continuous 27-minute trace." A careful dome defender can rescue with "the chart shows temporal correlation between Boulder's magnetometer and a globally-averaged GRACE measurement during the same UT window, not spatial co-location." detail_extra objection (ii) handles this, but the rebuttal lives only in the anticipated-objections paragraph that a typical reader will not click through to. The c1 recommendation (option a) was: "Add one sentence to detail_evidence point (3) inline-acknowledging the temporal-correlation rescue and noting that (a) phase-locked is the dome's own term and (b) even the temporal-only reading collides with L1A telemetry timing conventions."
holes_addressed.fix: Single appended-sentence patch implementing option (a) exactly. The rescue is named ("a temporal correlation between Boulder's magnetometer and a globally-averaged (not spatially co-located) GRACE measurement") and closed on the two grounds c1 specified: (i) "phase-locked" is the dome's own term and denotes spatial-temporal coupling, making the strictly-temporal rescue self-contradictory with the dome's adjacent framing; (ii) even the temporal-only reading requires reconciliation with GRACE L1A timing conventions (epoch tags, sampling cadence, downsampling schedule per JPL D-22027), which the chart neither addresses nor cites. Option (b) — refactoring point (3) to lead with the 27-minute infeasibility — is rejected as more invasive and unnecessary because the 27-minute-window argument is already the strongest leg and the appended sentence adds the rescue closure as a corollary rather than restructuring the paragraph.

new_evidence_added: No new sources or numerical claims — the patch only names a rescue path explicitly and points back to the already-cited JPL D-22027 L1A product specification (the timing-reconciliation argument is a consequence of the L1A product spec the WIN already relies on for point (1)).

anticipated_objections.objection: A defender could reply: "phase-locked is dome rhetoric, not a load-bearing claim — drop the rhetorical flourish and the temporal-correlation rescue stands clean."
anticipated_objections.response: Granted as rhetoric, but the rescue would then have to disclaim the dome's own home-page framing of Exhibit A. That is itself a confession: the home-page exhibit's headline claim ("phase-locked downward gravitational pulse correlated with the magnetic excursion") would be acknowledged as marketing-grade overstatement rather than a description of the data. Even if the defender absorbs that cost, leg (ii) — the L1A telemetry-timing reconciliation absent from the chart — survives independently. The original 27-minute-window infeasibility argument is preserved verbatim in the same paragraph and does not depend on the temporal-rescue closure at all.

anticipated_objections.objection: A skeptic could argue: "adding ~860 characters to a single point in an already-long paragraph dilutes the chart's structural failure (the L1A/µGal label collision in point (1)). The whole detail_evidence body now skews toward orbital-mechanics minutiae."
anticipated_objections.response: Considered the other side (per user preference): the chart's structural attack surface has five points (label collision, naming error, orbital infeasibility, absent code, absent AI formula). Adding 860 chars to point (3) leaves point (3) at roughly proportionate weight (the longest of the five points is point (4) on the repo audit; point (3) post-patch is comparable). The rescue-closure is not minutiae — it is the path a careful defender would actually take, and leaving it only in detail_extra invites a "you only address spatial co-location; we meant temporal" rebuttal that wastes review iterations. The patch is surgical (single appended sentence, two ground-clauses) rather than restructured (option b in the c1 recommendation), which was the explicit lower-cost choice.

anticipated_objections.objection: A reader could note: "the patch cites JPL D-22027 as the authority on L1A timing conventions, but the citation has dead-link risk per WIN-070.c1 hole #7. Building a load-bearing rescue-closure on a fragile citation is poor practice."
anticipated_objections.response: Fair concern. The patch already pre-empts this by including the PO.DAAC GRACE_L1A_GRAV_JPL_RL03 dataset landing page as a backup citation in parenthetical form. The landing page is the same authority promoted to backup status per c1 hole #7's recommendation. If JPL D-22027 rots, the landing page suffices to ground the timing-reconciliation claim. A follow-up EXP (queued by c1 hole #7) should add the Bettadpur 2018 product-specification document (CSR/GRACE 327-720) or the GRACE Level-1 Software User Manual (JPL D-32865) as a third-tier anchor; that work is out of scope here but is flagged in unaddressed_holes_note below.

kernel_of_truth: No change to the existing kernel-of-truth framing of WIN-070 (the top panel's INTERMAGNET BOU trace is real and correctly identified). The patch operates entirely within the "everything below the magnetometer trace fails" portion of the argument and refines point (3) without disturbing the kernel paragraph.
cross_references: WIN-012 (Self-Contradicted) — κ undefined under WIN-013/014 nulls; not touched by this EXP.
WIN-058b (Not Demonstrated) — κ not derived from dome geometry; not touched by this EXP.

detail_extra anticipated-rescue objection (ii) on globally-averaged GRACE Level-3 — left intact; the appended sentence in detail_evidence pre-empts the rescue inline rather than replacing the detail_extra coverage.
unaddressed_holes_note: EXP-422 scope is strictly WIN-070.c1 moderate hole on orbital-mechanics framing (ISS-2074). The other WIN-070.c1 holes addressed in companion EXPs or remaining open: (a) the Exhibit B unfalsifiability hole (moderate) is addressed in EXP-423 (parallel patch — point 6 insertion); (b) the verdict-label hole (moderate, "Refuted by Data" vs "Misleading"/"Self-Contradicted") is intentionally NOT addressed here — c1 recommended either re-tagging or defending the current label in detail_verdict_text, and the current detail_verdict_text already includes the orbital-mechanics defense ("independent GRACE orbital parameters refute the depicted 27-minute window before any labeling or code-absence argument is needed"), so the verdict-label question is best treated separately by the decider; (c) c1 minor holes 4-7 (inline rebuttal of L1A-shorthand rescue in point 1, post_hoc tag rationale tightening, audit-methodology disclosure in point 4, backup citation for JPL D-22027) are NOT addressed here and remain candidates for a polish EXP; (d) c1 stronger-arguments candidates (dome's own claim_index.json κ=pending_contested; 10^21x gravito-magnetic scale mismatch; WIN-034 symmetry argument against AI=1.152) are independently valuable and would meaningfully strengthen the WIN but are intentionally deferred to keep this EXP's scope tight.
notes_for_decider: One atomic sentence-scope append to WIN-070.detail_evidence point (3) in data/wins.json. BEFORE string verified unique (count=1) on 2026-05-17. No new outbound domains: both JPL URLs cited in the appended sentence (podaac-tools.jpl.nasa.gov L1A spec and podaac.jpl.nasa.gov landing page) are already present in detail_evidence point (1) and detail_verdict_text. Character delta: +978. Projected new detail_evidence length: 6905 (current 5927). TLDRs, detail_verdict_text, finding, and code_analysis are intentionally unchanged. UTF-8 em-dashes (—) used — preserve UTF-8 throughout. After integration: (a) run tests; (b) confirm both outbound links resolve (they are already in the WIN, so the test is a sanity check rather than a new validation); (c) consider integrating EXP-423 (parallel Exhibit B patch) in the same decider pass for a consistent WIN-070 expansion cycle.
deeper_analysis:
The orbital-mechanics argument is the single load-bearing data-based attack in WIN-070 (the other four points are taxonomic / definitional / repo-absence arguments). Leaving the temporal-correlation rescue closed only in detail_extra weakens the most quantitatively-anchored leg of the attack. The patch is short, surgical, and follows the c1 recommendation verbatim (option a).

Considering the other side (per user preference): a strict purist could argue that adding rescue-closures inline is a form of "writing for the dome defender" and risks an arms race where every conceivable rescue gets a pre-emptive paragraph, bloating the WIN. The counter is that this specific rescue (temporal-only over spatial-only) is documented in c1 as the actual likely defender move, not a hypothetical — closing it inline is responsive to the specific attack surface, not preemptive bloat. The patch also explicitly leaves the four minor c1 holes for separate work, so the scope-discipline argument is satisfied.

Note on the broader κ-cluster: WIN-070 sits at the third surface of the κ attack (WIN-012 attacks the derivation, WIN-058b attacks the geometry, WIN-070 attacks the home-page presentation). The patch makes the WIN-070 attack surface more robust to defender rescue without changing the cross-WIN structure.
tldr_change: None. The tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict for WIN-070 do not reference the temporal-correlation rescue — they cover the L1A/µGal label collision, the orbital infeasibility (with the 27-minute framing that survives the patch), the absent code, and the orbital-mechanics conclusion. The TLDR review standard ("Fix factual errors. Do not split hairs on nuance — the expanded detail handles that") is satisfied: the TLDR remains accurate and the rescue-closure is appropriate for the expanded detail rather than the punchline-first summary.
